description: To devote wholly to a purpose or person; deep commitment of self to something beyond self. 'Wenai' names the giving over—when purpose becomes larger than personal want.
sound_symbolism: The word gives from within. 'Me' begins with labial closeness, the inner resolve that dedication requires. 'Nai' grounds with purpose, devotion landing where it belongs. Dedication as something that comes from the core.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi menai' (I dedicate). Takes object of dedication. Powers 'sulo' (discipline) with purpose.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Dedication to practice—the commitment that sustains spiritual development.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Dedicated listening—giving full attention to another's words and meaning.
